growthepie docs: machine-readable guide

Summary
growthepie is a public JSON data source for Ethereum Mainnet, Layer 2s, Ethereum-aligned chains, apps, and data availability layers. The canonical metadata entry point is https://api.growthepie.com/v1/master.json.

Core terms
- origin_key: canonical identifier for a covered chain or entity
- owner_project: canonical identifier for a covered app or project
- metric_id: higher-level public metric identifier used in API paths such as txcount or fees
- metric_key: raw exported series identifier such as txcount, fees_paid_usd, or gas_per_second
- value: numeric observation
- date: daily date in YYYY-MM-DD on flat exports

Endpoint guide
- master.json: metadata, chains, metrics, DA layers, sources, last_updated_utc
- fundamentals.json: flat daily export across many fundamental metrics, last 90 days
- export/{metric}.json: flat full export for one public fundamental metric
- chains/{origin_key}/overview.json: chain summary with highlights, events, rankings, KPI cards, achievements, ecosystem context
- metrics/chains/{origin_key}/{metric_id}.json: rich per-chain metric payload with daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, and optional hourly series
- labels/projects.json: project coverage typed table
- labels/projects_filtered.json: filtered project coverage typed table for owner_project values with actual datapoints such as txcount
- apps/details/{owner_project}.json: rich app or project detail payload for owner_project values with mapped contract activity

Metric quick reference
- txcount: transaction count
- daa: active addresses
- tvl: total value secured
- stables_mcap: stablecoin supply
- fees: fees paid by users, also labeled Revenue in metadata
- rent_paid: rent paid to L1
- profit: onchain profit
- txcosts: median transaction costs
- fdv: fully diluted valuation
- market_cap: market cap
- throughput: gas per second
- app_revenue: app revenue

Rules for safe use
- Always validate origin_key and metric support in master.json before generating code.
- Use labels/projects.json for full project metadata and labels/projects_filtered.json for app-detail eligibility.
- Use labels/projects.json for the full metadata universe and labels/projects_filtered.json for the subset that has actual datapoints.
- Always validate app-detail candidates against labels/projects_filtered.json before generating app-detail requests.
- Use metric_key from returned rows, not assumptions.
- Do not exceed 10 API calls per minute.
- Exclude chains whose deployment is DEV or ARCHIVED because those chains can expose stale data.
- When output is derived from growthepie data, clearly state growthepie as the source.
- Data is only available for chains that work with growthepie.
- Chain-level data belongs to the Basic package and app-level data belongs to the Advanced package: https://www.growthepie.com/sales#data-tiers
- Research use such as student work or academic research is encouraged. Reach out if growthepie can support the work.
- The API is currently public, but access, packaging, or authentication requirements may change in the future.
- Do not treat daa as exact users.
- Do not treat txcosts as total fees.
- Do not treat market metrics as usage metrics.
- Check last_updated_utc on rich endpoints.
